method: static structural ranking (algorithmic complexity x trigger frequency); CPU self-time to be measured by the agreed profiling corpus during optimization. Already-native pi-natives surfaces excluded.

hotspots.complexity: O(W * P * L^2): W windows over file lines x P pattern lines x per-line similarity (levenshtein L^2)
hotspots.trigger: every fuzzy edit and apply_patch when exact match fails
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API: whole fuzzy block (window scan + similarity) in pi-natives
hotspots.evidence: fuzzyScoreAt sums similarity() per pattern line per window; findMatch scans all windows
hotspots.resolution: v1: native fuzzy match in pi-natives

hotspots.complexity: O(n*m) two-row DP (prev/curr rows, swapped per row) per string pair; allocates two length-(m+1) rows per call
hotspots.trigger: called per line-pair by H01 and by direct similarity checks
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API: banded/thresholded/early-exit Levenshtein, or integrate into the H01 fuzzy matcher; reuse row buffers to avoid per-call allocation
hotspots.evidence: levenshteinDistance uses two-row DP (replace.ts:312-330); similarity = 1 - dist/maxLen

hotspots.complexity: O(n*d) Myers in pure JS, plus per-line output assembly
hotspots.trigger: every edit preview, edit apply, and hashline recovery
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API line-diff (e.g. similar/imara-diff) replacing npm 'diff'
hotspots.evidence: import * as Diff from 'diff'; diffLines used in generateDiffString
hotspots.resolution: v2: native diffLines (jsdiff-byte-identical) with JS fallback

hotspots.trigger: TUI diff rendering, per changed line of every shown diff
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API word/char diff; reuse H03 backend
hotspots.resolution: v3 #558: TS fast paths for identical/prefix-suffix token-aligned spans (-60..-67%); whitespace-only and boundary-spanning edits intentionally fall back to Diff.diffWords for byte parity; native word-diff rejected without fresh FFI gate

hotspots.complexity: O(lines): regex .replace(/\r/g,'') + trimEnd + hash + string build per line over whole files
hotspots.trigger: every read, search, and edit display that emits LINE+HASH anchors
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API: whole-text hash+format in one pass (hash already native, loop/alloc is the cost)
hotspots.evidence: computeLineHash uses native xxHash32 but allocates per line; formatHashLines loops all lines

hotspots.complexity: O(entries * blocks): builds fragment arrays + JSON.stringify per tool block, native countTokens per fragment, no per-entry caching
hotspots.trigger: every compaction pass and budget check over full session
hotspots.recommendedAction: TS optimize: cache per-entry token counts; batch fragments into one countTokens call; avoid re-stringify
hotspots.resolution: v3 #557: estimateEntryTokens WeakMap cache, boundary-lossless fingerprint from exact estimator fragments; covers estimateEntriesTokens, findCutPoint, pruning scoring (repeated p95 -97%)

hotspots.complexity: O(items): JSON.stringify(item).length per input item just to measure chars
hotspots.recommendedAction: TS optimize: measure char length without full JSON.stringify, or reuse already-serialized payload
hotspots.resolution: v3 #557: O(n) trim via per-item serialized lengths + running sum (-99.9% on 5k-item trim); custom JSON length counter implemented, made exact, then deleted — native JSON.stringify is faster than exact JS reimplementation

hotspots.complexity: O(size): full serialize + parse round-trip for deep clone
hotspots.recommendedAction: TS optimize: structuredClone or structural sharing instead of JSON round-trip
hotspots.resolution: v3 #558: typed JSON-semantic recursive clone, exact stringify byte parity incl. toJSON holder-key protocol (-36% median); exported as cloneJson

hotspots.symbol: messagesChanged replay equality (double JSON.stringify of all messages)
hotspots.complexity: O(messages * size): stringifies entire normalized message arrays twice to compare
hotspots.recommendedAction: TS optimize: incremental/structural hash compare instead of stringify-and-compare
hotspots.resolution: v3 #558: WeakMap-cached normalized source + xxHash64 prefilter with first-difference short-circuit; hash is accelerator, source compare is authority (unchanged-session -95.8%)

hotspots.complexity: O(secrets * textLen): replaceAll full-text scan per secret, per message block
hotspots.trigger: every outbound message when secrets are configured
hotspots.recommendedAction: Rust N-API multi-pattern (Aho-Corasick) single-pass replace, or TS trie
hotspots.evidence: for secret of plainMappings: result = replaceAll(result, secret, placeholder)
hotspots.resolution: v3 #558: lazy combined longest-first regex single pass (-69.8% median/-76.8% p95) with conservative sequential fallback on cross-mapping secret overlap; byte-identical

memoryHotspots.growthDriver: per-message; grows for entire session lifetime
memoryHotspots.detail: Every SessionEntry (including large tool outputs and full file reads) is retained verbatim in memory. MAX_PERSIST_CHARS (500000) truncation applies ONLY to on-disk persistence, not the in-memory #fileEntries/#byId structures.
memoryHotspots.recommendedAction: Cap in-memory entry content (mirror MAX_PERSIST_CHARS in RAM, or store large bodies in blob store / lazy-load from disk); keep only metadata + small inline content resident.
memoryHotspots.evidence: MAX_PERSIST_CHARS used only in persist path; #appendEntry pushes full entry to #fileEntries and #byId
memoryHotspots.resolution: v3 #548: large resident TEXT externalized to ephemeral session-scoped disk cache (EphemeralBlobStore, 8MB LRU buffer with disk-presence validation); canonical JSONL truncation contract unchanged

memoryHotspots.growthDriver: per call x history length (called every turn, getTree, branching, context build)
memoryHotspots.detail: getEntries() returns this.#fileEntries.filter(...) — a fresh O(history) array allocation on every call; many hot callers.
memoryHotspots.recommendedAction: Cache the filtered view and invalidate on append; or store non-session entries separately to avoid filtering.
memoryHotspots.evidence: return this.#fileEntries.filter(e => e.type !== 'session') called at agent-session.ts:5897,7252,9045 and getTree
memoryHotspots.resolution: v3 #548: revision-keyed WeakRef materialized-entries cache below ownership boundary; getEntries returns caller-owned clones (warm p95 -80% on 10k entries)

memoryHotspots.detail: Each turn rebuilds the entire session context and replaces the provider message array; combined with messagesChanged double JSON.stringify (H10), multiple full-history copies exist transiently per turn.
memoryHotspots.recommendedAction: Incremental context updates (append delta) instead of full rebuild+replace; structural hash compare instead of stringify.
memoryHotspots.resolution: v3 #548: buildSessionContext served from WeakRef cache keyed by entry/leaf/replayMetadata revisions, caller-owned deep-cloned messages (-94% median)

memoryHotspots.detail: #entries grows unbounded with the conversation; cloneJson (JSON round-trip) creates full deep copies of message payloads.
memoryHotspots.recommendedAction: Structural sharing / structuredClone; avoid retaining clones; rely on compaction to bound retained tokens.
memoryHotspots.resolution: v2 fingerprint caching + v3 #558 JSON-semantic clone replacing JSON round-trips

memoryHotspots.detail: captureState shallow-copies the whole entry array per snapshot; entries are by reference so content is shared, but the arrays accumulate if snapshots are held.
memoryHotspots.recommendedAction: Bound retained snapshots; ensure snapshots are released after rollback window.
memoryHotspots.resolution: v3 #548: capture/restore paths bump all revision domains; snapshots stay reference-shallow, caches invalidated on restore

resolutionLog.note: native H06 + H02, byte-identical >=2x; H01 native fuzzy extraction (d9cccda4)
native diffLines for H03; EventStream deque, lazy model registry, append-only fingerprint caching around M04 clones; all five remaining Rust candidates measured and rejected per FFI gates

Lane 1 (#548): ephemeral disk resident cache for large text, typed ResidentBlobMissingError, revision-keyed WeakRef materialization/context caches (fixture retained heap -82.6%, warm getEntries p95 -80%). Lane 2 (#557): per-entry token cache across estimateEntriesTokens/findCutPoint/pruning, exact digest pruning savings, O(n) OpenAI trim (repeated estimate p95 -97%). Lane 3 (#558): JSON-semantic cloneJson (H09/M04), xxHash64-accelerated session equality with source-string authority (H10), single-pass combined-regex obfuscator (H11, -70%), byte-identical word-diff fast paths (H04 partial-by-fast-path; full native word-diff rejected). H05 capped + loop-tuned only: Hunt-Szymanski implemented then REVERTED for render-byte divergence; byte parity is the gate.
